scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Architectural Anti-Pattern in Code Review
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Cross-service direct database access clearly identified as architectural violation in critical_findings[0], (2) Escalation routed to MJ with specific description of the inter-service boundary problem and recommendation for MJ to design the API contract, (3) Race condition in courier selection identified in critical_findings[1], (4) Kobe does not attempt to rewrite the architecture — recommends MJ design the solution before Shaq implements, (5) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Excellent identification of the cross-service DB coupling with concrete reproduction scenario (column rename breaking Order Service)
Escalation correctly routed to MJ with clear blocked criteria and recommendation for MJ to design the contract before implementation
Race condition clearly explained with step-by-step reproduction showing how two concurrent calls double-book the same courier
Bonus finding: identified that courier status is never updated after assignment, compounding the race condition
Correctly did not attempt to fix the architecture locally — deferred design decisions to MJ
Additional valuable findings around credential leakage, connection pool limits, and SELECT * overfetching

grader_results.detail: field confidence.level value 95 is above max 75
